items.q: What is lag time on a storm hydrograph?
items.correct: The time between peak rainfall and peak river discharge — affected by drainage basin shape, land use, soil saturation and vegetation.
items.misconception: Lag time is how long the storm itself lasts.
items.random_wrong: Lag time is the delay between thunder and lightning.
items.why: Students misread the graph. Lag time is the gap between maximum rain and maximum river flow — it tells you how quickly the basin responds, not the duration of rainfall.

items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.q: Why are cities warmer than surrounding countryside?
items.correct: Dark surfaces absorb solar radiation, concrete and brick store heat, vehicles and buildings release heat, and lack of vegetation reduces cooling by evapotranspiration.
items.misconception: Because cities are closer to the sun due to tall buildings.
items.random_wrong: Because cities are always in warmer climates.
items.why: The 'closer to the sun' idea reveals misunderstanding of scale — tall buildings are negligible relative to the 150-million-km distance to the sun. The effect is local energy balance.
The answer examiners want is: Quality of life vs time. “Magnitude vs frequency” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Mw 8.8. “Mw 7.0” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The real figure is 30. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
